commodities.name: couscous
terms: couscous from cereals other than durum wheat
couscous from coarse and fine semolina mixture

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
1.1 The term “couscous”, as defined in Section 2 below, refers to processed couscous destined for direct human consumption.
1.2 Subject to the provision of Section 8.1.2 this standard does not apply to couscous intended for the same use but prepared from cereals other than durum wheat.

1.1 Coarse semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 475 and 700 microns.
1.2 Fine semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 130 and 183 microns.
1.3 “Coarse medium semolina” is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 183 and 700 microns.
1.4 Medium semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 183 and 475 microns.

1.2 Subject to the provision of Section 8.1.2 this standard does not apply to couscous intended for the same use but prepared from cereals other than durum wheat.

1.1 Coarse semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 475 and 700 microns.
1.2 Fine semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 130 and 183 microns.
1.3 “Coarse medium semolina” is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 183 and 700 microns.
1.4 Medium semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 183 and 475 microns.

page_spans.text:
1.1 The term “couscous”, as defined in Section 2 below, refers to processed couscous destined for direct human consumption.
1.2 Subject to the provision of Section 8.1.2 this standard does not apply to couscous intended for the same use but prepared from cereals other than durum wheat.

1.1 Coarse semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 475 and 700 microns.
1.2 Fine semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 130 and 183 microns.
1.3 “Coarse medium semolina” is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 183 and 700 microns.
1.4 Medium semolina is a semolina with a kernel diameter between 183 and 475 microns.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text:
2.1 This standard applies to couscous, i.e. the product prepared from durum wheat semolina ( Triticum durum) the elements of which are bound by adding pot able water and which has undergone physical treatment such as cooking and drying.
2.2 Couscous is prepared from a mixture of coarse and fine semolina. It can be prepared from “coarse medium” semolina.

2.1 Semolina proportions in the mixture intended for the preparation of couscous are:
– 20–30% for fine semolina
– 70–80% for coarse semolina

“Coarse medium” semolina is a semolina obtained from a mixture of:
– 25–30% for coarse semolina
– 70–75% for medium semolina

sections.title: ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
sections.text:
3.1 Quality factors – general
3.1.1 Couscous shall be clean, safe and fit for human consumption.
3.1.2 All processes applied to materials used for the production of couscous must be carried out in order to:
(a) limit the reduction of nutritive value
(b) avoid undesirable modification of properties of couscous

3.1 Granularity (microns) min. 630 microns to max. 2000 microns, with a tolerance of 6%.
3.2 Ash(%)  maximum 1.1%

3.2 Ash(%)  maximum 1.1%

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text:
No food additives shall be added during the industrial processing of couscous.

4.1 Ash
ISO 2171-1980, Cereals, Legumes and Derived Products – Determination of ash.

sections.title: 6.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
path_titles: 6.3 WHEN TESTED BY APPROPRIATE METHODS OF SAMPLING AND EXAMINATION, THE PRODUCT:
sections.text: (a) shall be free from micro-organisms capable of development in the food under normal conditions of storage; and (b) shall not contain any substance originating from micro-organisms in amounts which may represent a health hazard.

sections.title: 7. PACKAGING
sections.text: 7.1 Couscous shall be packaged for retail sale in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional and technological qualities of the product. 7.2 Containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They shall not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
sections.retrieval_text:
7 PACKAGING
number=7
level=1
7.1 Couscous shall be packaged for retail sale in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional and technological qualities of the product. 7.2 Containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They shall not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.

page_spans.text: 7.1 Couscous shall be packaged for retail sale in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional and technological qualities of the product. 7.2 Containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They shall not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.

sections.title: 8. LABELLING
sections.text: In addition to the provisions of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply: 8.1 Name of the product 8.1.1 The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “couscous”. 8.1.2 Products intended for the same use but prepared from cereals other than durum wheat may be designated as “couscous” on condition that this appellation be immediately followed by a specification of the cereals used. 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers Information for non-retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
sections.retrieval_text:
8 LABELLING
number=8
level=1
In addition to the provisions of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply: 8.1 Name of the product 8.1.1 The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “couscous”. 8.1.2 Products intended for the same use but prepared from cereals other than durum wheat may be designated as “couscous” on condition that this appellation be immediately followed by a specification of the cereals used. 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers Information for non-retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

page_spans.text: In addition to the provisions of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply: 8.1 Name of the product 8.1.1 The name of the product to be shown on the label shall be “couscous”. 8.1.2 Products intended for the same use but prepared from cereals other than durum wheat may be designated as “couscous” on condition that this appellation be immediately followed by a specification of the cereals used. 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers Information for non-retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sentences.original: 2.1 This standard applies to couscous, i.e. the product prepared from durum wheat semolina ( Triticum durum) the elements of which are bound by adding pot able water and which has undergone physical treatment such as cooking and drying.

classification.sentence:
2.1 Semolina proportions in the mixture intended for the preparation of couscous are:
– 20–30% for fine semolina
– 70–80% for coarse semolina

“Coarse medium” semolina is a semolina obtained from a mixture of:
– 25–30% for coarse semolina
– 70–75% for medium semolina
classification.pattern: Specification of percentage ranges for ingredients in mixtures

components.coarse_medium_semolina_definition: obtained from mixture of 25–30% coarse semolina and 70–75% medium semolina

classification.justification: Provides quantitative limits for ingredient proportions in mixtures, a limits and targets pattern not matched by known templates.

sentences.original:
3.1 Quality factors – general
3.1.1 Couscous shall be clean, safe and fit for human consumption.

classification.sentence:
3.1.2 All processes applied to materials used for the production of couscous must be carried out in order to:
(a) limit the reduction of nutritive value
(b) avoid undesirable modification of properties of couscous

3.1 Granularity (microns) min. 630 microns to max. 2000 microns, with a tolerance of 6%.
3.2 Ash(%)  maximum 1.1%
classification.function: Mandate for process objectives and specification of quality limits
classification.pattern: Obligation specifying process objectives followed by limits for physical and chemical parameters

process_obligations: limit the reduction of nutritive value
avoid undesirable modification of properties of couscous
components.granularity_limits: min. 630 microns to max. 2000 microns with 6% tolerance

classification.justification: Combines normative mandates on process execution with quantitative limits on product parameters; new composite pattern.
notes: Multiple obligations and limit specifications in single statement.

sentences.original: 8.2 Labelling of non-retail containers Information for non-retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container.

classification.sentence: However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR COUSCOUS
pages.summary: Página 1 do documento 'STANDARD FOR COUSCOUS' apresenta as seções principais e subseções da norma Codex Standard 202-1995 para couscous. Títulos numerados hierarquicamente do nível 1 ao 3 estão claramente identificados, com textos associados a cada seção e subseção conforme o conteúdo da página. A estrutura inclui seções como SCOPE, DESCRIPTION, ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS (com subdivisões como Quality factors – general e específicos), FOOD ADDITIVES, CONTAMINANTS e HYGIENE. A página não apresenta continuidade de seções em outras páginas.

pages.title: Codex Standard 202-1995
pages.summary: A página 2 do documento apresenta seções numeradas do nível 1, iniciando no meio da seção 6 (continuação da página anterior). As seções 6.2 e 6.3 têm texto explicativo associado e são continuação da página anterior. As seções 7 e 8 são totalmente iniciadas nesta página. A seção 8 indica continuidade para a próxima página. Não foram identificadas subseções detalhadas hierarquicamente nesta página devido à limitação do texto apresentado.

pages.title: Codex Standard 202-1995
pages.summary: Página com 4 seções principais numeradas (nível 1) relacionadas a especificações de semolina: Kernel Sizes, Composition, Quality Factors e Analysis. Texto interno associado detalha descrições e limites. Estrutura clara e sem continuação entre páginas nesta página.
